sampling.scope: schema and aligned-modality content parsing; split manifests and repository metadata may be read outside the sample only for split/provenance discovery
sampling.candidate_discovery: Recursively enumerate absolute paths below dataset_root without opening content. Exclude any path containing a .git segment or ending in .metadata. Define a modality family as (lowercase extension, absolute parent directory). Among .png families choose the family with the greatest file count, likewise .svg and .npy; among .json families choose the two families with the greatest file counts. Ties are broken by ordinal lexical absolute parent path. Candidate sample keys are basename-without-final-extension values present exactly once in each of those five selected families.
sampling.rank: SHA256(UTF8('M12_ARCHCAD_W3|' + sample_key)); ascending hexadecimal digest, then ordinal lexical sample_key
sampling.malformed_or_missing_sample_policy: retain the selected key in denominators and record the failed modality; do not replace it

measurement_policy.file_census: A dataset-wide absolute-path metadata census is permitted; no dataset file is modified.
measurement_policy.sample_content: Open only the five files belonging to each selected sample key. PNG may be header-decoded, SVG/XML and JSON parsed, and NPY loaded with allow_pickle=false. Record parse failures as evidence.
measurement_policy.metadata_and_split_surfaces: README files, dataset cards, repository metadata, and explicit split manifests may be read. Claims from prose are corroborating evidence only and cannot by themselves PASS schema or coordinates.
measurement_policy.unknown_policy: Preserve UNKNOWN. No missing label, split, coordinate, or SEG-IR field may be invented.

schema.pass: At least 50 candidate keys exist; the sealed sample size equals min(500,candidate_count); at least 95% of sampled keys parse in all five selected modality families; a machine-readable class vocabulary or unambiguous label values are extracted from parsed sample content; numeric/string label-to-class interpretation is internally consistent with no more than 1% unexplained observed label values; a wall class is present and its machine-readable representation is identified; and alignment by sample key plus compatible per-sample geometry/canvas metadata is verified for at least 95% of fully parsed samples.
schema.fail: Any measured contradiction makes the claimed five-family alignment or class mapping unsafe: fewer than 90% of sampled keys parse in all five families, more than 1% unexplained observed label values, conflicting meanings for the same class identifier, wall absent from the measured machine-readable vocabulary/labels, or geometry/canvas alignment contradiction in more than 5% of fully parsed samples.
schema.inconclusive: Neither PASS nor FAIL: fewer than 50 candidates, 90% to below 95% full parse, labels or wall semantics not machine-decodable without an observed contradiction, or alignment cannot be tested from available fields.

split_isolation.pass: An official machine-readable train/val/test assignment exists; every measured sample assignment is unique; a machine-readable project/drawing grouping key exists; and the complete manifest or complete path census shows zero project/drawing identifiers shared by different splits.
split_isolation.fail: No official train/val/test assignment exists, any sample has multiple split assignments, any project/drawing identifier crosses splits, or the official assignment is demonstrably random/sample-level while multiple samples from the same project/drawing cross splits.
split_isolation.inconclusive: An official assignment exists but no reliable project/drawing grouping key can be extracted, or only a partial manifest is available and no leakage is observed in the available evidence.

coordinates.pass: At least 50 selected samples yield numeric coordinate-bearing data; at least 95% parse successfully; unit, origin, axis order/directions, and scale are established from machine-readable fields plus measured values; p05/median/p95 span or scale statistics are reported; and a single explicit ArchCAD-to-SEG-IR normalization transform covers at least 95% of coordinate-parsed samples with cross-modal extent residual no greater than 5% of the larger span.
coordinates.fail: Measured coordinate conventions conflict without a deterministic discriminator, at least 10% of parsed aligned samples require mutually incompatible transforms, non-finite/degenerate coordinates occur in more than 5% of coordinate-bearing sampled files, or cross-modal extent residual exceeds 5% in more than 10% of comparable samples.
coordinates.inconclusive: Neither PASS nor FAIL: fewer than 50 coordinate-bearing samples, below 95% coordinate parse, physical unit or axis direction cannot be established from machine-readable evidence, or a required cross-modal transform cannot be evaluated.

adapter_feasibility.pass: A deterministic source-to-SEG-IR field/geometry mapping is specified for all source semantics evidenced by the sample; every required SEG-IR core field has a source rule, derived rule, or explicit UNKNOWN representation; coordinate normalization is executable from the measured parameters; all information-loss points and unsupported fields are enumerated; and no unresolved item can silently corrupt geometry, class identity, instance identity, wall topology, or split provenance.
adapter_feasibility.fail: A required SEG-IR geometry/class/instance/wall-topology field is contradicted or cannot be represented without silent corruption, or the measured coordinate convention admits no deterministic normalization.
adapter_feasibility.inconclusive: A mapping design can be drafted but one or more blocking target-schema, source-semantic, coordinate, topology, or provenance requirements remain UNKNOWN without a measured contradiction.

overall_verdict.PARTIAL: no axis FAIL and one or more axes INCONCLUSIVE
